name: Potato Skins with Salsa and Cheese
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Diced, seeded, firm, ripe tomatoes
1/4 cup Rinsed, drained canned black beans
1 tbsp Finely chopped green bell pepper
1 tsp Seedless, finely chopped jalapeno or other pepper
2 tbsp Shredded part-time mozzarella or reduced-fat Monterey Jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Wash potatoes thoroughly and pierce with a knife. Bake directly on the oven rack until tender (about 45 minutes). Let cool.
3. Meanwhile, make salsa: In a bowl, combine diced tomatoes, corn, black beans, cucumber, scallion, 2 tablespoons olive oil, green bell pepper, cilantro, lime juice, and jalapeno. Add salt to taste.
4. Halve potatoes lengthwise and carefully remove all but 1/8"-1/4" of the potato, leaving the skin intact. Discard insides for another use.
5. Brush inside of potato shells lightly with olive oil. Preheat broiler.
6. Place potato skins cut side up on a baking sheet and broil 3-4 inches from flame for 5 minutes. Turn skins over and broil other side for 1 minute.
7. Spoon salsa into potato skins, dividing evenly. Sprinkle with cheese, dividing evenly. Broil until cheese is melted.
8. Serve immediately.
